task_completion.description: Create a horizontal timeline with at least 6 events connected by arrows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas shows a horizontal sequence of at least 6 event nodes arranged from left to right, with arrows pointing between them to indicate chronological order.

task_completion.description: Include timestamps in the event labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text labels for the events on the timeline include time values (e.g., 18:00, 23:27, 18:14).

task_completion.description: Highlight the 'rm -rf' event in red using a distinctive shape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event corresponding to 'rm -rf' (at 23:27) is contained within a distinctive shape (like a Callout, Star, or Cloud, rather than a standard rectangle) and is colored red.

task_completion.description: Highlight the 'Service Restored' event in green.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event corresponding to 'Service Restored' (at 18:14) is colored green.
The draw.io application title bar shows 'incident_timeline.drawio', or there is a save dialog/file manager showing this file saved in the Diagrams folder.

task_completion.description: Export the diagram as a PDF named 'incident_timeline.pdf'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog, download notification, or file manager shows 'incident_timeline.pdf' being saved or existing in the Diagrams/exports/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io application open with the diagram being edited on the canvas, rather than a terminal window running a script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The diagram consists of individual shapes and text elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface shows selection handles, grid lines, or toolbars indicating that the diagram is composed of editable shapes, not just a single pasted image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The incident log contains 8 distinct events. The critical 'rm -rf' event occurred at 23:27 UTC. The 'Service Restored' event occurred at 18:14 UTC. The timeline must have at least 6 events. The 'rm -rf' event must be red and use a non-standard shape (e.g., Callout, Star, Cloud). The 'Service Restored' event must be green.
